title: barringtonia acutangula subsp. acutangula
intro: Barringtonia acutangula subsp. acutangula is a plant species native to tropical Asia, also known as the 'Jewels of the Sea' or 'Sea Poison', which gets its name from its toxic seeds and attractive flowers. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its leaves, roots, and bark, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Barringtonia acutangula subsp. acutangula include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, making it effective in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. The herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and poultices, often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
